title: Parablechnum chauliodontum
taxonomy: Parablechnum chauliodontum is classified as a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that belongs to the genus Parablechnum. The Blechnaceae family is a group of ferns commonly known as chain ferns. This family comprises about 200 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Parablechnum chauliodontum is endemic to the tropical forests of Central and South America.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, likely due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
